steps.title: Split the absolute value into two equations
steps.content:
$|18-2x|=66$ means the expression inside equals either $66$ or $-66$:
Case 1: $18-2x=66$
Case 2: $18-2x=-66$

steps.title: Solve each case for $9-x$
steps.content:
Case 1: $18-2x=66 \Rightarrow -2x=48 \Rightarrow x=-24 \Rightarrow 9-x=9-(-24)=33$.

Case 2: $18-2x=-66 \Rightarrow -2x=-84 \Rightarrow x=42 \Rightarrow 9-x=9-42=-33$.

The two possible values of $9-x$ are $33$ and $-33$.

steps.title: Match to choices
steps.content:
• A: $0$ — neither solution gives $9-x=0$. Eliminated.
  • B: $9$ — neither solution gives $9-x=9$. Eliminated.
  • C: $33$ — Case 1 gives $9-x=33$. C is correct.
  • D: $48$ — neither solution gives $9-x=48$. Eliminated.
